one_line_takeaway: Task-adaptive, LLM-generated rubrics with per-dimension confidence-weighted scoring beat fixed-rubric LLM-as-Judge for agent-trajectory evaluation (r 0.79 vs 0.64) and yield DPO/PPO reward signals that lift agent success by up to +8.5 points; its DimensionAwareFilter provably blocks dimension-level quality masking, a reward-integrity property adjacent to reward hacking.
key_finding: Across WebArena, ToolBench and AgentBench, ADARUBRIC-DA reaches Pearson r = 0.79/0.74/0.77 with human expert rankings (avg 0.77, +0.15 over the strongest baseline GPT-4 Direct at 0.64/0.60/0.62), with reliability Krippendorff alpha of 0.85/0.82 (DA variant, avg 0.84; abstract rounds the headline to ~0.83). A compute-matched GPT-4 CoT-Decomposed baseline reaches only r=0.68 on WebArena, evidencing the gain comes from task-adaptive rubrics rather than extra test-time compute. DPO on ADARUBRIC-DA pairs improves downstream task success by +6.8/+8.5/+7.7 points over the Prometheus baseline (WA 27.8 SR%, TB 37.8 TCR%, AB 34.1 SR%). It generalizes zero-shot to SWE-bench Lite (r=0.77, DPO resolve rate 14.7%, +4.9 over GPT-4 Direct) and to multimodal agents (VisualWebArena r=0.76, OSWorld r=0.73), and as a PPO reward reaches 30.2% WebArena SR at 5K rollouts (+6.6 over Prometheus) with faster convergence.
core_contribution: ADARUBRIC is a framework (single author, Liang Ding) that replaces the fixed-rubric LLM-as-Judge paradigm with task-adaptive evaluation of LLM agent trajectories. Its three claimed-novel components are: (1) adaptive rubric generation (Stage 1) that prompts an LLM to synthesize N task-specific, orthogonal, weighted evaluation dimensions with calibrated 5-point criteria directly from a task description, cached per task type; (2) confidence-weighted, per-step per-dimension scoring (Stage 2) producing dense reward signals for post-training (DPO/PPO); and (3) an end-to-end evaluation-to-training pipeline (Stage 3) with composable filters, including the novel DimensionAwareFilter and an accompanying masking-prevention theorem (Proposition 3.1) proving that a single scalar/global threshold can always be passed by a trajectory whose one dimension scores near zero, whereas per-dimension thresholding cannot be so gamed. It also repurposes Krippendorff's alpha (a borrowed inter-rater metric) as an inter-run reliability/deployment criterion (alpha >= 0.80) for LLM evaluators.
threat_model: The paper presents no adversarial threat model in its core contribution; it frames the work as evaluation reliability and reward-signal quality, not security. Its relevance to the reward-hacking theme is a reward-integrity mechanism rather than a defense against an attacker: DimensionAwareFilter + Proposition 3.1 provably prevent 'dimension-level quality masking,' i.e., a trajectory that scores high on some dimensions to mask a near-zero score on another while still clearing a global/scalar threshold (Remark 1 example: Search=5, Extract=5, Reason=1 gives S=3.8, passing theta=3.5, yet fails reasoning). The only genuinely adversarial content is a limitations pilot: because rubrics are generated from the task description, adversarially crafted descriptions could bias evaluation; on 30 perturbed descriptions Pearson r degrades by 0.04-0.06 (bounded but non-trivial). No attacker capabilities, no defense evaluation beyond that pilot.
limitations: Rubric quality depends on evaluator LLM capability; vague/underspecified tasks yield incomplete or overlapping dimensions, and generation can miss specialized criteria experts include (e.g., CAPTCHA Handling, Rate-Limit Awareness). Adversarially crafted task descriptions bias evaluation (r drops 0.04-0.06 on a 30-description pilot). Confidence scores are LLM-predicted and may need recalibration OOD. Cost is K x N evaluator calls per trajectory (~40 for WebArena) at 3-5x GPT-4 Direct wall-clock; caching amortizes only rubric generation, not per-step evaluation. The BLUE justification is an idealization (heavy-tailed residuals). The human study is modest (300 pairs/benchmark, three annotators); multimodal/PPO/SWE-bench sections are deliberately compact generalization evidence. Single-pass evaluation could be improved by multi-round verification at higher cost.

datasets_used: SWE-bench Lite (300 sampled trajectories, code-repair generalization)
quantitative_results: Human correlation Pearson r (ADARUBRIC-DA): WA 0.79 / TB 0.74 / AB 0.77 (avg 0.77) vs GPT-4 Direct 0.64/0.60/0.62 (+0.15 avg)
Compute-matched GPT-4 CoT-Decomposed baseline r=0.68 on WebArena (vs ADARUBRIC-DA 0.79)
Krippendorff alpha (ADARUBRIC-DA): WA 0.85 / TB 0.82 (avg 0.84); headline rounded to ~0.83; all pass deployment threshold alpha>=0.80 while G-Eval 0.63 and Prometheus 0.70 fail
DPO downstream success (ADARUBRIC-DA, Qwen2.5-7B): WA 27.8 SR% / TB 37.8 TCR% / AB 34.1 SR%, i.e. +6.8/+8.5/+7.7 over Prometheus
SWE-bench Lite: r=0.77 vs oracle, alpha 0.84, DPO resolve rate 14.7% (+4.9 over GPT-4 Direct)
PPO reward (WebArena SR%): ADARUBRIC-DA 20.1/27.4/30.2 at 1K/3K/5K rollouts, +6.6 over Prometheus at 5K, +4.3 at 1K (faster convergence)
Multimodal: VisualWebArena r=0.76 / OSWorld r=0.73 (+0.14 over GPT-4V Direct); +5.8% DPO SR gain
Ablation (WebArena): generic fixed rubric r=0.51 -> full ADARUBRIC-DA r=0.79 (+0.28 cumulative; adaptive generation +0.07, confidence weighting +0.03, DimensionAwareFilter +0.04)
Filter comparison (WebArena): DimensionAwareFilter best DPO SR 27.8 while retaining 61.5% of pairs; CompositeFilter over-filters (26.9, 47.2% retained)
N=5 dimensions optimal; recency-decay lambda=0.5 optimal (r 0.79 vs 0.72 at lambda=2.0)
Rubric-quality human study (Likert 1-5, Rel/Orth/Comp): ADARUBRIC 4.3/4.2/4.1 vs Expert-designed 4.6/4.4/4.5 vs Generic 2.1/3.8/1.6
Cross-domain transfer: ADARUBRIC WA->TB 31.2% exceeds Prometheus in-domain TB 29.3%
Adversarial-description pilot: r degrades 0.04-0.06 on 30 perturbed descriptions
Confidence-weighting empirical variance reduction 18-24% vs uniform averaging
Calibration: ADARUBRIC score buckets vs human percentile ranks Spearman rho=0.98
baselines_compared: GPT-4 Direct (single-turn no rubric, r 0.62 avg; primary reference)
DPO baselines: random pairing, SFT on successful trajectories, DPO-G-Eval, DPO-Prometheus
PPO reward baselines: GPT-4 Scalar, Prometheus reward
